title: Piquant Egyptian Sirloin Wrap
description: A jammy, flaky, date-night Egyptian dish featuring sirloin steak, stone-bowl-dolsot, and a lotus-leaf wrap, fodmap-friendly and bursting with spice.
instructions: Season the sirloin steak with salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika and a drizzle of olive oil.
Heat a stone-bowl-dolsot over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Sear the steak for 2-3 minutes per side until browned, then remove and let rest.
In the same dolsot, sauté jasmine rice with a splash of garlic-infused oil until lightly toasted.
Stir in the low-sodium soy sauce, lemon juice and minced ginger, cooking until the rice is fragrant.
Slice the rested steak into thin strips and scatter them over the rice in the dolsot.
Warm lotus leaves over a low flame until pliable, then spoon the rice-stone mix onto each leaf.
Fold the lotus leaves into wraps and serve immediately with a side of fresh cucumber slices.
